addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

5	This prompt is self-contained — start here.

7	---

10

12	packages/web/src/plan/PlanCanvas.tsx                — main plan interaction handler
13	packages/web/src/tools/toolGrammar.ts               — per-tool grammar state machines
14	packages/web/src/plan/planElementMeshBuilders.ts    — dispatches plan symbols per element
15	packages/web/src/groups/groupCommands.ts            — applyCreateGroup, applyPlaceGroup etc.
16	packages/web/src/groups/groupTypes.ts               — GroupDefinition, GroupInstance types

19	packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ts           — 3D mesh dispatcher
20	packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts              — ToolId union + TOOL_REGISTRY
21	```
22	
23	Tests: co-located `*.test.ts` — run `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`.
24	Prettier runs automatically after every Edit/Write.
25	
26	---

28	## What wave 1 already built — DO NOT rebuild these
29	
30	- `ArrayState` / `reduceArray` grammar in `toolGrammar.ts` — 14 tests, linear + radial

37	
38	---
39	
40	## Tasks
41	
42	### B1 — Array tool PlanCanvas wiring
43	
44	The grammar (`ArrayState`/`reduceArray`) is complete but not dispatched from PlanCanvas.
45	Find `case 'scale':` in PlanCanvas.tsx and add a parallel `case 'array':` block that:
46	- Routes mousedown/mouseup/mousemove/keydown events through `reduceArray`
47	- Passes `activeTool === 'array'` to the relevant sections (copy exactly how scale does it)
48	- On the grammar's `effect.kind === 'createLinearArray'` or `'createRadialArray'`, dispatch
49	  the corresponding semantic command through the command queue
50	
51	Also wire the options bar for array: show "Count" number input and "Linear / Radial" toggle.

56	Update tracker §3.3.6 Array: "Implemented (WP-B wave 2)"
57	
58	---
59	
60	### B2 — Group plan renderer
61	
62	Create `packages/web/src/plan/groupInstanceRender.ts`:
63	- For each `group_instance` element in the current view, compute the bounding rectangle
64	  of all definition-member elements at their transformed positions
65	- Render a dashed rectangle in plan at the group instance position
66	- When the instance is selected, render with selection highlight colour
67	- Export a function `buildGroupInstancePlanMesh(instance, registry, scene)` following
68	  the same pattern as other plan symbol builders
69	
70	Call this from `planElementMeshBuilders.ts` for elements with `kind === 'group_instance'`.
71	
72	Tests:
73	- Given a GroupInstance and a GroupDefinition with two walls, buildGroupInstancePlanMesh
74	  returns a dashed rectangle geometry at the correct position
75	
76	---
77	
78	### B3 — Group 3D renderer
79	
80	Create `packages/web/src/viewport/groupInstance3d.ts`:
81	- For each `group_instance` element, retrieve its `GroupDefinition` from the registry
82	- Apply the instance's insertion point + rotation as a Three.js Matrix4
83	- For each element in the definition, call the existing mesh builder for that element type
84	  at the transformed position
85	- Return a Three.js Group containing all child meshes
86	
87	Dispatch this from `meshBuilders.ts` for `kind === 'group_instance'`.

89	---

91	### B4 — Place-group grammar
92	
93	Add `PlaceGroupState` / `reducePlaceGroup` to `toolGrammar.ts`:
94	- `idle` → user clicks in plan → `{ type: 'placeGroup', definitionId, positionMm }`
95	- The options bar shows a dropdown of available group definitions from `groupRegistry`
96	  (pass registry into the grammar or read from store in PlanCanvas handler)
97	- Wire in PlanCanvas.tsx `case 'place-group':` following the scale/array pattern

102	
103	In `ProjectBrowser.tsx` (or `ProjectBrowserV3.tsx`), add a "Groups" tree node:
104	- Lists all `GroupDefinition` entries from `groupRegistry` by name
105	- Shows instance count as a badge: "Wall Cluster (3)"
106	- Right-click context menu: "Rename", "Select All Instances", "Delete"
107	- Study the "Families" subtree as the exact pattern to follow
108	
109	---

file.content:
# Wave 2 — WP-B: Array PlanCanvas Wiring + Group Renderers

You are an agent working on the **bim-ai** repo (`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`).
bim-ai is a browser-based BIM authoring tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest).
This prompt is self-contained — start here.

---

## Repo orientation

```
packages/web/src/plan/PlanCanvas.tsx                — main plan interaction handler
packages/web/src/tools/toolGrammar.ts               — per-tool grammar state machines
packages/web/src/plan/planElementMeshBuilders.ts    — dispatches plan symbols per element
packages/web/src/groups/groupCommands.ts            — applyCreateGroup, applyPlaceGroup etc.
packages/web/src/groups/groupTypes.ts               — GroupDefinition, GroupInstance types
packages/web/src/state/                             — Zustand store slices
packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.tsx — project browser tree
packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ts           — 3D mesh dispatcher
packages/web/src/tools/toolRegistry.ts              — ToolId union + TOOL_REGISTRY
```

Tests: co-located `*.test.ts` — run `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`.
Prettier runs automatically after every Edit/Write.

---

## What wave 1 already built — DO NOT rebuild these

- `ArrayState` / `reduceArray` grammar in `toolGrammar.ts` — 14 tests, linear + radial
- `'array'` ToolId in toolRegistry.ts
- `GroupRegistry` (definitions + instances) in Zustand store (`StoreState.groupRegistry`)
- `CreateGroupDialog.tsx` wired to `model.create-group` palette command (Cmd+K)
- `applyCreateGroup`, `applyPlaceGroup`, `applyUngroupElements` in `groupCommands.ts` — 21 tests
- `'place-group'` ToolId in toolRegistry.ts
- Scale tool PlanCanvas wiring is DONE — **study `case 'scale':` in PlanCanvas.tsx as the pattern**

---

## Tasks

### B1 — Array tool PlanCanvas wiring

The grammar (`ArrayState`/`reduceArray`) is complete but not dispatched from PlanCanvas.
Find `case 'scale':` in PlanCanvas.tsx and add a parallel `case 'array':` block that:
- Routes mousedown/mouseup/mousemove/keydown events through `reduceArray`
- Passes `activeTool === 'array'` to the relevant sections (copy exactly how scale does it)
- On the grammar's `effect.kind === 'createLinearArray'` or `'createRadialArray'`, dispatch
  the corresponding semantic command through the command queue

Also wire the options bar for array: show "Count" number input and "Linear / Radial" toggle.
Study how the scale tool's options bar works.

Tests: confirm `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web -- arrayTool` still passes after wiring.

Update tracker §3.3.6 Array: "Implemented (WP-B wave 2)"

---

### B2 — Group plan renderer

Create `packages/web/src/plan/groupInstanceRender.ts`:
- For each `group_instance` element in the current view, compute the bounding rectangle
  of all definition-member elements at their transformed positions
- Render a dashed rectangle in plan at the group instance position
- When the instance is selected, render with selection highlight colour
- Export a function `buildGroupInstancePlanMesh(instance, registry, scene)` following
  the same pattern as other plan symbol builders

Call this from `planElementMeshBuilders.ts` for elements with `kind === 'group_instance'`.

Tests:
- Given a GroupInstance and a GroupDefinition with two walls, buildGroupInstancePlanMesh
  returns a dashed rectangle geometry at the correct position

---

### B3 — Group 3D renderer

Create `packages/web/src/viewport/groupInstance3d.ts`:
- For each `group_instance` element, retrieve its `GroupDefinition` from the registry
- Apply the instance's insertion point + rotation as a Three.js Matrix4
- For each element in the definition, call the existing mesh builder for that element type
  at the transformed position
- Return a Three.js Group containing all child meshes

Dispatch this from `meshBuilders.ts` for `kind === 'group_instance'`.

---

### B4 — Place-group grammar

Add `PlaceGroupState` / `reducePlaceGroup` to `toolGrammar.ts`:
- `idle` → user clicks in plan → `{ type: 'placeGroup', definitionId, positionMm }`
- The options bar shows a dropdown of available group definitions from `groupRegistry`
  (pass registry into the grammar or read from store in PlanCanvas handler)
- Wire in PlanCanvas.tsx `case 'place-group':` following the scale/array pattern

---

### B5 — Groups subtree in ProjectBrowser

In `ProjectBrowser.tsx` (or `ProjectBrowserV3.tsx`), add a "Groups" tree node:
- Lists all `GroupDefinition` entries from `groupRegistry` by name
- Shows instance count as a badge: "Wall Cluster (3)"
- Right-click context menu: "Rename", "Select All Instances", "Delete"
- Study the "Families" subtree as the exact pattern to follow

---

## Rules

- `git pull --rebase origin main` before editing `toolGrammar.ts` or `PlanCanvas.tsx`
  (other wave-2 agents also touch these files)
- Commit + push after each completed task
- DO NOT touch IFC export, curtain wall, attach grammar, annotation grips, or core/index.ts
- `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web` before each push
- Update `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md` as you complete items

360:- **C6:** Ctrl+C/V wired. copyToLevels.ts + pasteAlignedToLevels fully implemented. PasteToLevelsDialog (Cmd+K → level-picker modal → dispatches copyElementsToLevels per target) complete with 8 passing tests. Dialog now wired in Workspace.tsx (openPasteToLevels palette context) — WP-B3 done.
373:#### 3.3.6 Gruppe »Ändern« (modify group: move, copy, rotate, mirror, array, scale, align, split, trim, offset, delete)
379:- Array (linear and radial): Partial — `arrayTool.ts` math helpers + `ArrayState`/`reduceArray` grammar in `toolGrammar.ts` (14 unit tests) are complete. **Missing: `PlanCanvas.tsx` dispatch case for `'array'` tool** — the grammar state machine exists but no click/key event bridge routes plan interactions through it. Next wave: add `case 'array':` to the PlanCanvas tool-event handler and wire the `createLinearArray`/`createRadialArray` semantic commands through the command queue.
380:- Scale: **Implemented (WP-B1)** — grammar (ScaleState/reduceScale, 12 tests) + full PlanCanvas.tsx wiring: click handler dispatches ScaleEvent phases (pick-origin → enter-factor → confirm/graphical), numeric input overlay with `×` suffix, phase instruction banner, Escape/cancel handling. Fires `scaleElement` semantic command on commit.
397:createSimilar.ts helper + CS shortcut in cheatsheet; PlanCanvas keyboard handler pending. Create Group: implemented as `model.create-group` Cmd+K command — opens `CreateGroupDialog.tsx` (name prompt + centroid origin) when ≥2 elements selected (WP-B2).
741:#### 8.5.2 Mehrere Geschossebenen mit Reihe-Funktion (add multiple levels with array)
1082:The family editor has a create workflow. Not all Revit family editor tools (extrusion, blend, revolve, sweep, swept blend, void forms) are fully available. ArrayTool.test.tsx exists.
1103:cheatsheetData.ts and CheatsheetModal.tsx provide a keyboard shortcut reference panel. bim-ai has hotkeys for most tools. The shortcut set does not yet match Revit's full keyboard shortcut schema (e.g. WA=Wall, DR=Door, WN=Window, CM=Copy, MM=Mirror, MV=Move, RO=Rotate, TR=Trim, SL=Split Line, AL=Align, OF=Offset, AR=Array, SC=Scale, GP=Group, UN=Ungroup, VV=Visibility Graphics, VP=View Properties, RP=Reference Plane, LL=Level, GR=Grid, DI=Aligned Dimension, DL=Linear Dimension, EL=Spot Elevation, TX=Text, TG=Tag).

1	/**
2	 * Per-tool grammar — spec §16.4 / §16.5.
3	 *
4	 * Each tool's interaction model is captured here as plain data so the
5	 * tool can be unit-tested without DOM. The shape is consistent across
6	 * tools so the canvas can switch on `kind` and dispatch input.
7	 */
8	
9	export type { WallLocationLine } from '@bim-ai/core';
10	import type { WallLocationLine } from '@bim-ai/core';
11	
12	/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
13	/* EDT-06 — Tool grammar polish (Chain / Multiple / Tag-on-Place /        */
14	/*           Numeric input). The canvas wires the per-tool reducers in    */
15	/*           this file plus a top-level `ToolGrammarModifiers` block      */
16	/*           that feeds the Options Bar.                                  */
17	/* ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
18	
19	/** Modifiers that the canvas Options Bar exposes to the user. */
20	export interface ToolGrammarModifiers {
21	  /** Place Wall continues from the last endpoint until Esc / different tool. */
22	  chainable: boolean;
23	  /** Insert Door / Window stays in tool until Esc; otherwise exits after first placement. */
24	  multipleable: boolean;
25	  /** During wall / door / window placement, auto-place a tag of the configured family. */
26	  tagOnPlace: { enabled: boolean; tagFamilyId?: string };
27	  /** Numeric input mode: typing a digit while drawing pops a numeric input field. */
28	  numericInputActive: boolean;
29	  /**
30	   * Wall draw offset — when non-zero, the wall baseline is drawn offset from
31	   * the cursor by this many mm (positive = left side of draw direction).
32	   * F-042 parity. Default 0.
33	   */
34	  wallDrawOffsetMm: number;
35	  /**
36	   * Wall draw radius — when non-null, consecutive wall segments get a curved
37	   * corner fillet of this radius (mm). F-043 parity. Default null (sharp corners).
38	   */
39	  wallDrawRadiusMm: number | null;
40	}
41	
42	export function defaultToolGrammarModifiers(): ToolGrammarModifiers {
43	  return {
44	    chainable: true,
45	    multipleable: false,
46	    tagOnPlace: { enabled: false },
47	    numericInputActive: false,
48	    wallDrawOffsetMm: 0,
49	    wallDrawRadiusMm: null,
50	  };
51	}
52	
53	/** A tool's static capability set — drives which Options Bar toggles appear. */
54	export interface ToolCapabilities {
55	  chainable: boolean;
56	  multipleable: boolean;
57	  tagOnPlace: boolean;
58	  /** Whether the tool supports typing a numeric distance while drawing. */
59	  numericInput: boolean;
60	}
61

190	/**
191	 * Numeric-input field state — appears at the cursor while a numeric-capable
192	 * tool is mid-draw and the user types a digit. `axis` toggles on Tab so a
193	 * second number can drive the perpendicular direction.
194	 */
195	export interface NumericInputState {
196	  active: boolean;
197	  value: string;
198	  axis: 'primary' | 'perpendicular';
199	}
200	
201	export function initialNumericInputState(): NumericInputState {
202	  return { active: false, value: '', axis: 'primary' };
203	}
204	
205	export type NumericInputEvent =
206	  | { kind: 'start'; firstDigit: string }
207	  | { kind: 'append'; digit: string }
208	  | { kind: 'backspace' }
209	  | { kind: 'tab-axis' }
210	  | { kind: 'commit' }
211	  | { kind: 'cancel' };
212	
213	export function reduceNumericInput(
214	  state: NumericInputState,
215	  event: NumericInputEvent,
216	): NumericInputState {
217	  switch (event.kind) {
218	    case 'start':
219	      return { active: true, value: event.firstDigit, axis: 'primary' };
220	    case 'append':
221	      if (!state.active) return state;
222	      return { ...state, value: state.value + event.digit };
223	    case 'backspace':
224	      if (!state.active) return state;
225	      return { ...state, value: state.value.slice(0, -1) };
226	    case 'tab-axis':
227	      if (!state.active) return state;
228	      return {
229	        ...state,
230	        axis: state.axis === 'primary' ? 'perpendicular' : 'primary',
231	      };
232	    case 'commit':
233	    case 'cancel':
234	      return initialNumericInputState();
235	  }
236	}
237	
238	export type ToolGrammarKind =
239	  | 'wall'
240	  | 'door'
241	  | 'window'
242	  | 'floor'
243	  | 'roof'
244	  | 'stair'
245	  | 'railing'
246	  | 'room'
247	  | 'dimension'
248	  | 'section'
249	  | 'tag'
250	  | 'align'
251	  | 'split'
252	  | 'trim'
253	  | 'offset'
254	  | 'wall-join';
255	
256	export const WALL_LOCATION_LINE_ORDER: WallLocationLine[] = [
257	  'wall-centerline',
258	  'finish-face-exterior',
259	  'finish-face-interior',
260	  'core-centerline',
261	  'core-face-exterior',
262	  'core-face-interior',
263	];
264

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

### 8.9 Gruppen verwenden (model groups)

#### 8.9.1 Gruppen erstellen (group selected elements)
**Status: Partial — P1**
`GroupRegistry` (definitions + instances) now in Zustand store (`StoreState.groupRegistry` + `setGroupRegistry`). `CreateGroupDialog.tsx` — modal dialog prompted by `model.create-group` Cmd+K palette command when ≥2 elements selected; computes centroid origin, validates name, calls `applyCreateGroup`, persists via `setGroupRegistry`. `model.ungroup` palette command removes a group instance from the registry. `groupCommands.test.ts` has 21 passing unit tests. **Still missing**: plan renderer for group bounding-box overlay (`plan/groupInstanceRender.ts`), Groups subtree in ProjectBrowser, 3D instance rendering.

#### 8.9.2 Gruppen einfügen (place a group instance)
**Status: Partial — P1**
`placeGroup` command shape + `applyPlaceGroup` logic exist in `groupCommands.ts`. `'place-group'` ToolId is registered in `toolRegistry.ts` and `tool.place-group` palette command activates the tool. **Still missing**: grammar state machine for the placement click sequence; options bar dropdown listing available group definitions; 3D viewport rendering of group instances.

#### 8.9.3 Gruppen bearbeiten (edit group contents)
**Status: Partial — P1**
`editGroup`/`finishEditGroup` command shapes exist in `groupCommands.ts`. **Missing**: edit-mode UI — ghosting non-group elements, restricting selection to group members, and "Finish Editing" button.

**Status: N/A**

attachment.snippet:
1	# Revit 2026 Feature-Parity Tracker
2	
3	Last updated: 2026-05-16
4	Source: Detlef Ridder — *Autodesk Revit 2026: Der umfassende Praxiseinstieg für Architekturkonstruktion*, mitp 2026 (ISBN 978-3-7475-1101-5)
5	
6	Purpose: exhaustive chapter-by-chapter comparison between Revit 2026 (as taught in the book) and what bim-ai currently supports. Every leaf section of the table of contents becomes a row. The goal is to expose gaps at maximum granularity so engineering work can be scoped and prioritised.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## Status Legend
11	
12	- `Done` — fully implemented, tested, and working at production quality
13	- `Partial` — some sub-capability exists but parity is incomplete
14	- `Not Started` — no implementation exists
15	- `N/A` — not applicable to a browser-based BIM SaaS (installation flows, desktop-only licensing, Windows hardware checks, etc.)
16	
17	Priority:
18	- `P0` — core authoring workflow; blocks architects from meaningful use
19	- `P1` — important for professional parity; significantly limits daily use if absent
20	- `P2` — useful but architects can work around it today
21	- `P3` — niche, advanced, or rarely used in typical architectural projects
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Chapter 1 — Revit installieren, starten und loslegen (UI & startup)
26	
27	### 1.1 Die Testversion (trial licensing)
28	**Status: N/A**
29	Revit requires download and 30-day activation. bim-ai is browser-based with its own subscription/trial model. No parity gap.
30	
31	### 1.2 Die Studentenversion (student licensing)
32	**Status: N/A**
33	Autodesk-specific educational licensing. bim-ai has its own account system.
34	
35	### 1.3 Hard- und Software-Voraussetzungen (system requirements)
36	**Status: N/A**
37	Revit 2026 requires 64-bit Windows 10/11, 16 GB RAM, DirectX 11 GPU, 30 GB disk. bim-ai runs in a browser. No parity gap.
38	
39	### 1.4 Installierte Programme (installed components)
40	**Status: N/A**
41	Revit installs Revit main, Viewer, Add-Ins Manager, Worksharing Monitor. bim-ai is a web app.
42	
43	### 1.5 Revit starten (startup & project templates)
44	**Status: Partial — P2**
45	Revit shows a start screen with recently-used projects, a choice of project template (None, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau vereinfacht, BIM Gebäudetechnik) and a new-project dialog. bim-ai has a project creation flow and project list, and now:
46	- Project templates (projectTemplates.ts) implemented: minimal, residential (Wohnbau), commercial (Gewerbebau) with level + phase setup commands. Template picker added to project creation dialog (ProjectSetupDialog.tsx, "Templates" section).
47	- Template picker allows selecting a template and applying its createLevel/createPhase commands in one click.
48	Still missing:
49	- No multi-template selection at project creation time (only one applied at a time)
50	- No "Revit 2026 start page" visual freshness (recently-used thumbnails, cloud projects)
51	- BIM Architektur vereinfacht template equivalent is missing
52	
53	### 1.6 Die Revit-Benutzeroberfläche (UI chrome)
54	
55	#### 1.6.1 Programmleiste (title bar with project + view name)
56	**Status: Partial — P2**
57	bim-ai shows current project name in the header. Current active view name is shown in the view tab. Full "Projekt1 - Grundriss: Ebene 0" combined title is not surfaced in a persistent title bar.
58	
59	#### 1.6.2 Dateimenü (file menu: New, Open, Save, Export, Print, Close)
60	**Status: Partial — P1**
61	bim-ai has:
62	- New project (Done)
63	- Open project (Done)
64	- Save / auto-save (Done)
65	- Export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
66	- Print / PDF export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
67	Missing: Save As template, Save to library as Family, cloud model sync from file menu, Revit Options dialog.
68	
69	#### 1.6.3 Schnellzugriff-Werkzeugkasten (quick access toolbar)
70	**Status: Partial — P2**
71	bim-ai has a fixed top bar with key actions (undo, redo, save, 3D toggle). Revit's QAT is fully user-customisable — any ribbon command can be pinned. bim-ai does not yet have a user-configurable quick access bar.
72	
73	#### 1.6.4 Die Info-Leiste (info bar: search help, Autodesk Account, App Store, Help)
74	**Status: Partial — P2**
75	bim-ai has a help/onboarding entry point but no in-product search over help documentation, no integrated App Store, no Autodesk Account management. The user profile/account menu exists.
76	
77	#### 1.6.5 Multifunktionsleiste, Register, Gruppen und Flyouts (ribbon with tabs, groups, flyouts)
78	**Status: Partial — P1**
79	Revit has a full ribbon with tabs: Architektur, Ingenieurbau, Stahlbau, Betonfertigteile, Gebäudetechnik, Einfügen, Beschriften, Berechnung, Körper & Grundstück, Zusammenarbeit, Ansicht, Verwalten, Zusatzmodule, Ändern. bim-ai has a compact vertical tool palette and a minimal top menu — no full ribbon architecture.
80	- Architecture tools (Architektur tab equivalent): Done for core tools; many sub-groups missing
81	- Structural tab (Ingenieurbau): Partial — columns and beams exist, no full structural ribbon
82	- Steel (Stahlbau): Not Started
83	- Precast (Betonfertigteile): Not Started
84	- MEP (Gebäudetechnik): Partial — duct/pipe/cable-tray/equipment in tool registry, no MEP-detail ribbon
85	- Insert (Einfügen): Partial — see Ch. 12
86	- Annotate (Beschriften): Partial — see Ch. 4
87	- Calculate (Berechnung): Not Started
88	- Mass & Site (Körper & Grundstück): Partial — see Ch. 11 & 5
89	- Collaborate (Zusammenarbeit): Partial — see collab features
90	- View (Ansicht): Partial
91	- Manage (Verwalten): Partial
92	- Add-ins (Zusatzmodule): N/A
93	- Modify (Ändern) context ribbon: Partial
94	
95	#### 1.6.6 Benutzung der Werkzeuge (how tools activate, options bar, modify ribbon)
96	**Status: Partial — P1**
97	bim-ai has a ToolPalette with hotkeys, OptionsBar, and ToolModifierBar. Revit-style "tool stays active until escape/finish" grammar is implemented. The chained-placement model (wall chain, stair by component, etc.) is done. Missing: the full floating options bar that changes per-tool is only partially implemented for some tools.
98	
99	#### 1.6.7 Exemplar- und Typeigenschaften, neue Elementtypen (instance vs type properties, type duplication)
100	**Status: Partial — P1**
101	bim-ai has the Inspector panel for element properties. Instance properties are shown and editable for walls, doors, windows, roofs, floors. Type properties and type duplication (WorkspaceRightRailTypeCommands.ts exists) are partially implemented. Creating entirely new types from scratch (e.g. new wall type with custom layer stack) is partially available in the wall type catalog. Full Revit-style "Typ bearbeiten" dialog with all type parameters is not yet fully parity.
102	
103	#### 1.6.8 Optionsleiste: wichtigste Exemplareigenschaften (options bar for active tool)
104	**Status: Partial — P1**
105	OptionsBar.tsx exists and is wired for wall placement (location line, chain mode, offset, radius). Not all tools have fully populated options bars as in Revit.
106	
107	#### 1.6.9 Statusleiste (status bar with command hints)
108	**Status: Partial — P2**
109	bim-ai shows contextual hints during active tool operations. Full Revit-style status bar with structured "Select element" / "Click to place" strings per tool state is not fully implemented.
110	
111	#### 1.6.10 Ansichtssteuerung (view controls: scale, detail level, visual style, shadows, crop)
112	**Status: Partial — P1**
113	bim-ai has:
114	- Plan detail level toolbar (PlanDetailLevelToolbar.tsx — Done)
115	- Visual style selection (renderStyles.ts — Done)
116	- Sun/shadow toggle (SunOverlay.tsx — Done)
117	- Scale display: Partial (shown in plan header)
118	Missing: per-view crop region editing, view-level visibility/graphics overrides dialog, thin-lines toggle ("Feine Linien"), hide/isolate element commands.
119	
120	#### 1.6.11 Projektbrowser (project browser tree: views, sheets, families, groups, Revit links)
121	**Status: Partial — P1 (D7)**
122	`ProjectBrowser.tsx` + `ProjectBrowserV3.tsx` implement the project browser. Done:
123	- Plan views grouped by discipline, sub-discipline, view type, and phase (F-032/F-099)
124	- Area Plans section with scheme buckets (F-098)

    feat(WP-NEXT-46): wire validateWorkPlane3d into 3D wall preview tinting
    
    When the snap lands on a wall-segment (not a level-plane), the wall
    draft preview now renders in red (#ef4444) instead of the accent blue,
    giving the user an immediate invalid-work-plane signal before click.
    
    - Import validateWorkPlane3d from authoring3d.ts in Viewport.tsx
    - tintWallDraftPreviewObject accepts optional overrideColor param
    - updateWallDraftPreviewGroup accepts optional tintColor param
    - Mouse-move handler calls validateWorkPlane3d('wall', snapKind, true)
      and passes '#ef4444' when previewTint === 'red'

commit 52d1ff244ff5bf2800ae7433601b7957d21563b9
Author: Johannes Hötter <jhoetter@MacBook-Pro-von-Johannes.local>
Date:   Sat May 16 10:23:14 2026 +0200

    feat(wp-b/B1): scale tool PlanCanvas wiring + interior-elevation scaffolding
    
    Scale tool (B1):
    - Import initialScaleState/reduceScale/ScaleState from toolGrammar + distanceMm/buildScaleCommand from scaleTool
    - scaleStateRef + scalePhase state added near rotate/copy refs
    - Tool-change useEffect activates scale grammar on planTool === 'scale'
    - Click handler: dispatches ScaleEvent { kind: 'click' }, executes commitScale/commitGraphicalScale effects via scaleElement command
    - Keyboard handler: digit/dot → set-input + numericInput update; Backspace → slice; Enter → confirm → commitScale
    - Escape handler: dispatches cancel, resets to pick-origin phase
    - Numeric overlay condition extended for scale enter-factor phase (shows factor × suffix)
    - scale-instruction banner shows per-phase prompt at bottom center
    
    Interior-elevation scaffolding (WP-NEXT carryover from stash):
    - interior_elevation_marker ElemKind + Element union variant in core/index.ts
    - interior-elevation ToolId + tool definition in toolRegistry.ts (plan-only, hotkey IE)
    - interior-elevation AUTHORING_COMMAND_CONTRACTS entry in authoringCommandContract.ts
    - tool.interior-elevation palette command + structural advisor commands in defaultCommands.ts
    - structural.delete-duplicate-wall + structural.detach-orphan capability entries
    - i18n keys + symbology entries
